the status section of the slot card has a lot of white space. I wonder if, instead of having a title, status row, and substatus row, if we collapsed it into one? Act as an HA designer when evaluating the current design

---

mock up option B, both with and without C flavored thinking

---

I like B+C, but why can't we adopt the secondary statuses from B only in B+C?

---

I guess we can get rid of blocked by conditions in B+C - didn't realize we moved it into the condition but I get it now

---

nevermind I like solution B, it's easier to read

---

what if we instead replaced the circular logo with the pill, and then used Active/inactive/disabled as a secondary status underneath the title? Inactive = Blocked by condiitons. Disabled = Slot is disabled by user, so we can use them interchangeably

---

yes

---

1. maybe we just move the SLOT 1 header to the left and put Alice's name immediately to the right?
2. it should also fire when locks are out of sync
3. yes

two more things:
1. let's see what conditions looks like when it's only the one condition - we are going to get rid of number of uses, might as well do it as part of this redesign
2. can you show me the expanded states of the cards too?

---

1. maybe we just move the SLOT 1 header to the left, have status underneath it, and put name to the right? Feel free to adjust if you have a better idea
2. it should also fire when locks are out of sync
3. yes

two more things:

2. can you show me the expanded states of the cards too?

---

1. maybe we just move the SLOT 1 header to the left, have status underneath it, and put name to the right? Feel free to adjust if you have a better idea
2. let's get rid of the scope. It's a lot of space for effectively one of four states (all is good, slot is disabled, slot is enabled but condition is active, slot is eanbled but not all locks are in sync)
3. yes

two more things:
1. let's see what conditions looks like when it's only the one condition - we are going to get rid of number of uses, might as well do it as part of this redesign

---

you misunderstood my intent on the header. The styling from before should have been kept with SLOT 1 and Alice in primary. To me the slot # is the organizational index that lives to the top left. And then the name and status become the most secondarily important things

---

why do they all have to be to the left is my point? there is so much white space now to the right. How would a designer leverage that full section knowing that we have these three pieces of information and their relative improtance. Youc an make styling adjustments, but make it make sense

---

SO much better

to answer your questions:
1. yes
2. sure, put the config entry title after it. That'll make it more useful on its own

OK now, how do we display condition helpers? We render them as entity rows correct? Do we do that for locks too? If so, we should do that for the condition entity

---

I think Path A compromise probably makes the most sense. Can you mock it?

---

1. it's just a config option on the entity row? keep it
2. let's rethink actions here and this space in general. It's not conditions, it's condition singular. Replace and delete are in two different areas and affect the config entry while editing edits the entities state. We need to rethink where we are putting what. Perhaps we need ot simplify what we offer here instead of trying to offer all of the editing capabilities. The nice thing about the UI approach is admins can give it to users to mess with
3. yes mock with both

---

1. manage condition is good but can we move it directly below the condition above the helpers themselves? Can you also mock up what that would open to?
2. actually it's a configuration on the card/strategy. There is no easy way to manage that via the frontend
3. that's fine. it's weird to have helpers without a condition, but I suppose users can do it that way if they want, what's the harm.

Finally, for the activity log, all we have is the entity event state and history. We should probably simplify what this does as cool as it looks

---

this is perfect. I would say when implementing that we want to use HA components where we can. Where we have to compose ourselves, let's be deliberate about what we compose versus what we don't. Do you want to create an implementation plan based on this mock? I foresee this happening as part 2 of this whole operation, with part 1 being to migrate config entries to remove number of uses, possibly create a repair issue, but otherwise clean up the code around number of uses

---

this is perfect. I would say when implementing that we want to use HA components where we can. Where we have to compose ourselves, let's be deliberate about what we compose versus what we don't - as an example, if we could, the condition entity picker should be an entity selector. Do you want to create an implementation plan based on this mock? I foresee this happening as part 2 of this whole operation, with part 1 being to migrate config entries to remove number of uses, possibly create a repair issue, but otherwise clean up the code around number of uses

Element ha-sidebar scheduled an update (generally because a property was set) after an update completed, causing a new update to be scheduled. This is inefficient and should be avoided unless the next update can only be scheduled as a side effect of the previous update. See https://lit.dev/msg/change-in-update for more information.

<task-id>a436b7458c63dbdbc</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01EP2E5k5hev8dq6Tx8nPd76</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-raman-projects-lock-code-manager/71c16e9b-de1a-419b-a20c-1b5f51ff2253/tasks/a436b7458c63dbdbc.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Accessibility review of PR #1116" completed</summary>
<result>Now I have a complete picture of the components. Let me organize my findings into the structured accessibility report.

# Accessibility Review — PR #1116 (slot-card / lock-codes-card redesign)

## Critical (WCAG violations / broken keyboard access)

### C1. Hero PIN value (`.hero-pin-value.editable`) is a click-only `&lt;span&gt;` — not keyboard accessible
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:532-539`
```tsx
&lt;span class="hero-pin-value editable ${...}" @click=${() =&gt; this._startEditing('pin')}&gt;
```
No `role`, no `tabindex`, no `@keydown`. A keyboard or screen-reader user cannot start editing the PIN at all. This is the most prominent action on the card. **Fix:** add `role="button"` `tabindex="0"` `aria-label="Edit PIN"` and a keydown handler that fires on Enter/Space — or (preferred) render an `&lt;button&gt;` since there's already a sibling pencil-style affordance for the name. WCAG 2.1.1.

### C2. Hero name value (`.hero-name-value.editable`) is also click-only
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:503-510`
The name `&lt;span&gt;` has only `@click`. The pencil `ha-icon-button` next to it (`slot-card.ts:511-516`) is keyboard-accessible and is functionally redundant — but the visual affordance is the editable text itself, and a keyboard user tabbing through the card jumps from the pencil button straight past the value with no indication that the text is interactive. **Fix:** mirror the pencil button's role, OR drop the `@click` from the span and rely on the pencil only — pick one keyboard-reachable trigger and remove the click-only one to avoid the dual-affordance trap.

### C3. Lock-card slot chip is a click-only `&lt;div&gt;` for managed slots
**File:** `ts/lock-codes-card.ts:507-514`
```tsx
&lt;div class="slot-chip ... ${clickableClass}" @click=${isClickable ? () =&gt; this._navigateToSlot(...) : nothing}&gt;
```
When `isClickable` is true the entire chip navigates to the LCM config entry, but there is no `role="button"`, no `tabindex`, no keydown handler, no `aria-label`. **Fix:** when `isClickable`, add `role="button"` `tabindex="0"` `aria-label="Manage slot ${slot.slot}"` and the Enter/Space keydown handler. WCAG 2.1.1.

### C4. Lock-card lock name is click-only (`.lock-name`) — slot card
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:916-922`
```tsx
&lt;span class="lock-name" title="View lock codes" @click=${() =&gt; this._navigateToLock(lock.lockEntityId)}&gt;
```
Same pattern: no role, tabindex, or keydown. Opens `hass-more-info` for the lock. **Fix:** same as C3 — `role="button"` + `tabindex="0"` + Enter/Space keydown, plus an `aria-label="View ${lock.name} more info"`.

### C5. Collapsible header is click-only and missing `aria-expanded`
**File:** `ts/shared-styles.ts:260-271` + `ts/slot-card.ts:973`
```tsx
&lt;div class="collapsible-header" @click=${onToggle}&gt;
```
The header that toggles the Conditions and Lock Status sections is a plain `&lt;div&gt;` with `cursor: pointer; user-select: none;` and only a `@click`. A keyboard user cannot expand or collapse those sections at all. The chevron icon is also not announced as an expand/collapse control. **Fix:** make it a `&lt;button&gt;` (or at minimum `role="button"` `tabindex="0"` with a keydown handler) AND add `aria-expanded="${expanded}"` and `aria-controls="..."` pointing at the collapsible-content's id. WCAG 2.1.1, 4.1.2.

### C6. Action error banner is not announced to screen readers
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:322-333`
The `.action-error` div appears dynamically when an action fails and disappears after 5s, but it has no `role="alert"` / `aria-live`. Assistive-tech users get no notification that the action failed. **Fix:** add `role="alert"` to the `.action-error` div (or `aria-live="assertive"`). WCAG 4.1.3.

### C7. "Saving..." dialog indicator is not announced
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:1106`
```tsx
${this._dialogSaving ? html`&lt;div class="dialog-saving"&gt;Saving…&lt;/div&gt;` : nothing}
```
Dynamically inserted/removed; no `aria-live`. **Fix:** add `aria-live="polite"` (or wrap with one). WCAG 4.1.3.

### C8. Lock-card slot chip click swallowed for keyboard? No — but reveal/edit buttons sit inside a clickable chip without `e.stopPropagation()` on every action
**File:** `ts/lock-codes-card.ts:507-556`
The chip gets `@click={isClickable ? navigate : nothing}`. Inside it the reveal `ha-icon-button` and the editable code span (for unmanaged slots) live without `stopPropagation`. For unmanaged slots `isClickable === false` so no conflict; for managed slots `isClickable === true` and there's no inline edit/reveal inside the same chip flow (the reveal button at line 637 only renders for masked-with-reveal mode — which DOES include managed slots, so a click on the eye also navigates away from the page). **Fix:** wrap the reveal button click with `e.stopPropagation()` on managed/clickable chips, or factor the chip-level navigation into a non-overlapping target. This isn't strictly WCAG but it's a broken interaction.

## Important (degraded experience for AT users)

### I1. State chip dot has no `aria-hidden`
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:468`, `ts/shared-styles.ts:90-95`
The decorative 6px (`.state-chip .dot`) and 5px (`.lcm-badge .dot`) dots are color-only redundancy; screen readers may read them as empty unlabelled items. **Fix:** since they are pure `&lt;span&gt;`s without text content, most screen readers ignore them, but adding `aria-hidden="true"` to the dot spans removes any ambiguity.

### I2. `aria-label` mislabeled on slot card "Edit name" pencil — it's `.label` (Material Web Components prop) but should also be the accessible name
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:515` — `.label=${'Edit name'}`
HA's `ha-icon-button` does forward `.label` to an `aria-label`, so this is fine in practice — but verify the same pattern at `slot-card.ts:545`, `:592-599`, `:943` (reveal/save/cancel/hide-codes). All looked correct on review. **Verified, no fix needed.**

### I3. Focus management for the Manage Condition dialog
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:1087-1109`
`&lt;ha-dialog open&gt;` from HA does handle focus trapping and restoration on close, but the dialog only contains an `ha-entity-picker` and the Saving indicator. There is no inline confirm/cancel button — the picker auto-commits on selection. When the dialog closes via auto-commit (`_closeConditionDialog` from `_commitConditionPick`), focus restoration is handled by `ha-dialog`. **Verified ha-dialog behavior, but please double-check at runtime** that pressing Esc in the picker returns focus to the Add condition button (at `slot-card.ts:608`) — the dialog's `@closed` handler runs but doesn't manually restore focus.

### I4. State chip uses color (success-green vs warning-orange vs disabled-gray) as the only differentiator besides text
The text labels ("Active", "Blocked by condition", "Disabled by user", "Unknown") at `slot-card.ts:457-467` carry the meaning, so this is OK. The `.lcm-code.off` vs `.lcm-code.pending` (shared-styles.ts:185-201) however have only a clock-outline icon prefix to distinguish "pending" from "off" — and the icon has no `aria-label`. **Fix:** add `aria-label="Pending sync"` to the `.lcm-code-pending-icon` `&lt;ha-svg-icon&gt;` at `slot-card.ts:543-546` and `lock-codes-card.ts:627-630`, OR add visually-hidden text inside the `.lcm-code.pending` span.

### I5. Collapsible badge "✓" / "✗" symbols are not announced semantically
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:629-631`
```tsx
&lt;span class="collapsible-badge ${isAllowing ? '' : 'warning'}"&gt;
    ${isAllowing ? '✓' : '✗'} ${name}
&lt;/span&gt;
```
Most screen readers read these glyphs as "check mark" / "ballot X", which is OK but not great. Consider replacing with `aria-label="Allowing access: ${name}"` on the parent badge, or substituting an `&lt;ha-svg-icon aria-hidden="true"&gt;` plus visually-hidden text.

### I6. Suspended banner missing `role="alert"`
**File:** `ts/lock-codes-card.ts:159-164`
The banner appears when sync is suspended — important state info. **Fix:** add `role="status"` (since it's not user-action driven, and it's persistent — `alert` would be too aggressive).

### I7. Edit input lacks `aria-label`
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:496-502` (name input) and `:523-531` (pin input)
The native `&lt;input&gt;` elements have no `&lt;label&gt;` element and no `aria-label`. The visible "Name" / "PIN" labels are sibling spans, not associated. **Fix:** add `aria-label="Edit name"` / `aria-label="Edit PIN"`, or use `aria-labelledby` pointing at the existing `.hero-field-label`.

### I8. Lock-codes-card edit input lacks accessible name
**File:** `ts/lock-codes-card.ts:577-587`
The `&lt;input class="slot-code-input" placeholder="PIN"&gt;` has only a placeholder. Placeholders are not accessible names. **Fix:** add `aria-label="Slot ${slot.slot} PIN"`.

### I9. Summary table missing caption / scope
**File:** `ts/lock-codes-card.ts:759-794`
The `&lt;table class="summary-table"&gt;` has `&lt;th&gt;` cells but no `&lt;caption&gt;` and no `scope="col"` / `scope="row"` attributes. Screen-reader users tabbing through cells get no row/column header context. **Fix:** add `&lt;caption class="visually-hidden"&gt;Code slot summary&lt;/caption&gt;` and `scope="col"` on the header cells, `scope="row"` on the first `&lt;td&gt;` of each body row.

### I10. Lock-card header is not a heading
**File:** `ts/lock-codes-card.ts:153-158`, `ts/slot-card.ts:418-433`
`.card-header-title` and `.header-title` are `&lt;span&gt;`s. Cards rendered in a Lovelace dashboard would benefit from a real `&lt;h2&gt;` or `&lt;h3&gt;` so screen-reader users can navigate by heading. (HA's stock cards do use heading elements.) **Fix:** render the title inside an `&lt;h2&gt;` or `&lt;h3&gt;` element.

### I11. Lock-card "View activity history" event row — same chip-inside-link pattern
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:376-394` — this one IS done correctly: `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, `aria-label="View activity history"`, keydown handler. **Verified.**

### I12. `remove-link` has a focus indicator AND keydown — verified
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:642-657` + `ts/slot-card.styles.ts:344-348`
`role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, `aria-label="Remove condition"`, keydown handler, AND a `:focus-visible` outline. **Verified, exemplary.** Use this pattern for the items in C1-C5.

## Nice-to-have (polish)

### N1. Touch target sizes
- `--mdc-icon-button-size: 28px` on the pencil (`slot-card.styles.ts:196`) and reveal buttons (`shared-styles.ts:244`) is below WCAG 2.5.5 (24x24 minimum at AA in 2.2 — passes) but below the 44x44 AAA recommendation. Consider 32px or use the default `ha-icon-button` size in compact contexts only.
- Dismiss `✕` button in the action error banner (`slot-card.ts:325-331`) has no explicit size — the default `&lt;button&gt;` with `font-size: 16px` and `padding: 4px` produces a roughly 24x24 hit target. Borderline. Consider increasing padding to 8px.

### N2. `prefers-reduced-motion` is ignored
**Files:**
- `ts/shared-styles.ts:307-322` — collapsible chevron rotates and content animates max-height 0.3s.
- `ts/lock-codes-card.styles.ts:117-127` — slot chip has hover translateY transition.
- `ts/slot-card.styles.ts:189` — `.editable` background transition.

**Fix:** wrap the transitions in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { ... }` or add a global override:
```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
    .collapsible-content, .collapsible-chevron, .slot-chip.clickable, .editable {
        transition: none !important;
    }
}
```

### N3. Color contrast — likely sub-AA combinations to verify against HA themes
Manual review against HA default themes (need browser testing for hard numbers, but flagging likely problems):
- **`.hero-field-label`** (`secondary-text-color`, 11px uppercase) on `--lcm-section-bg` (3% primary text) — at 11px this is "normal text" and needs 4.5:1. HA's default `--secondary-text-color` is ~`#727272` on light theme; 4.5:1 is borderline.
- **`.lcm-code.off`** (`disabled-text-color` ~`#9e9e9e`) on `--lcm-section-bg` — definitely below 4.5:1. Already in a "disabled" pill, so per WCAG inactive UI elements are exempt — but the dimmed PIN dots also use this and are conveying a state, not an inactive control. Reconsider the color choice or add a non-color indicator.
- **`.lock-synced-time`** (11px secondary-text) — same concern as the field label.
- **`.summary-cell-zero`** (`disabled-text-color` `!important`) — applied to numeric "0" cells. The number conveys data, not just decoration; a non-zero "1" in primary color next to a "0" in disabled color is hard for low-vision users. Consider keeping primary text color and using only font weight to de-emphasize.
- **`.action-error`** (`color: white` on `var(--error-color, #db4437)`) — `#db4437` white = 4.21:1, below AA for normal text. Use a darker red like `#b00020` or add `font-weight: 600`.
- **`.add-condition-btn:hover`** flips to `color: var(--text-primary-color, #fff)` on `background: var(--primary-color)` — depends on user's theme; verify against the default blue.

### N4. Helpers list and lock list could be `&lt;ul&gt;`/`&lt;li&gt;`
**Files:** `ts/slot-card.ts:728-740` (helpers), `:846-848` (lock rows)
Currently rendered as a sequence of `&lt;div&gt;`s. Wrapping in `&lt;ul&gt;` with `&lt;li&gt;` children gives screen-reader users an item count ("List, 3 items"). Low priority.

### N5. Conditions section title should also be a heading inside the collapsible
The "Condition" / "Lock Status" titles inside the collapsible header (`ts/slot-card.ts:974`) are `&lt;div&gt;`s. Consider `&lt;h3&gt;` to support heading navigation.

### N6. `.dialog-content` has no `&lt;h1&gt;`/`&lt;h2&gt;` for the dialog heading
**File:** `ts/slot-card.ts:1089` — the heading is set via `ha-dialog`'s `.heading=${'Add condition'}` property. That should map to a heading internally; HA's dialog does expose it via the dialog title slot, so probably fine. **Likely OK.**

## Verified (no issues found)

- **Activity event row** (`ts/slot-card.ts:376-394`): proper `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, `aria-label`, keydown for Enter/Space, focus-visible style at `slot-card.styles.ts:526-530`. Exemplary.
- **Remove condition link** (`ts/slot-card.ts:642-657`): same pattern, correctly implemented.
- **Add condition button** (`ts/slot-card.ts:606-620`): native `&lt;button&gt;`, `aria-label="Add condition"`, focus-visible style at `slot-card.styles.ts:378-381`. Good.
- **Action error dismiss button** (`ts/slot-card.ts:325-331`): native `&lt;button&gt;` with `aria-label="Dismiss error"`. Good.
- **Reveal eye buttons** (`ts/slot-card.ts:541-546`, `:939-944`, `lock-codes-card.ts:637-642`): use `ha-icon-button` with `.label=${this._revealed ? 'Hide PIN' : 'Reveal PIN'}` (toggling label is best practice). Good.
- **Save/Cancel `ha-icon-button`s in lock-card edit mode** (`lock-codes-card.ts:589-600`): proper `.label` props. Good.
- **Focus on input on edit-mode entry**: `slot-card.ts:236-253` and `lock-codes-card.ts:194-198` both have explicit `.focus()` + `.select()` lifecycle hooks. Good.
- **Pin reveal-on-edit flow** (`slot-card.ts:1169-1192`): properly waits for the resubscribe to land before flipping `_editingField`, so the focused input has the real value at focus time.
- **Code display modes** (masked / unmasked) communicate state via the eye button's label change AND the visible character change — not color-only. Good.

## Top-priority fix recommendations

1. **C1, C2, C3, C4, C5** — broken keyboard access on the most-used clickable elements. These are full WCAG 2.1.1 violations.
2. **C6, C7, I6** — add `role="alert"` / `aria-live` to error and saving indicators.
3. **I7, I8, I9, I10** — accessible names on inputs and proper headings.
4. **N2** — `prefers-reduced-motion` opt-out.

The codebase already demonstrates the right pattern in two places (event row, remove link), so the same approach can be lifted to fix C1-C5 with very low risk.</result>
<usage><total_tokens>86358</total_tokens><tool_uses>6</tool_uses><duration_ms>121017</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

<task-notification>
<task-id>a79c367387c753598</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01B9bgqgHP9GhH6o89DKwwNr</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-raman-projects-lock-code-manager/71c16e9b-de1a-419b-a20c-1b5f51ff2253/tasks/a79c367387c753598.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Design + IA review of PR #1116" completed</summary>
<result>I've gathered enough. Now, let me synthesize the design review.

---

# Design review: PR #1116 — Slot card and lock card redesign

## Strengths

- **Cross-card state language is genuinely unified.** The state-chip definition in `slot-card.styles.ts:87-134` and the badge-state definition in `shared-styles.ts:73-119` use identical color tokens, identical 16% tints, identical 6px/5px dot pattern, and identical sentence-case treatment. This is the kind of consistency that quietly pays off — a state badge reads the same in either card.
- **The `Slot N · {entry_title}` pattern is consistent.** Slot card kicker (`slot-card.ts:435-439`) and lock card chip label (`lock-codes-card.ts:516-522`) both use the middle-dot separator, both surface the entry title in `--secondary-text-color`. Good.
- **Removing the destructive button from the dialog and pulling Remove inline below the condition block is the right call.** It matches the principle "destructive actions live next to their subject," reduces dialog cognitive load to one decision (which entity?), and the auto-commit picker collapses Save+Cancel into "select = done."
- **Two-state PIN treatment (`.lcm-code.off` muted pill vs `.lcm-code.pending` clock prefix)** — this carries real semantic weight without strikethrough or alarming color. Good restraint. Mirroring it on the slot name (`slot-chip.disabled .slot-name`, `slot-chip.pending .slot-name`) ties the row together visually.
- **The condition block (HA entity row + LCM overlay) is a clever Path A compromise** — HA's domain rendering on top, slot-specific framing below. It avoids reinventing calendar/schedule rendering while still answering "what does this mean for *this slot*."
- **Event row is honest about the data we have.** The earlier mockup hinted at a richer activity log; pulling it back to a single click-through row with `Last used: &lt;lock&gt; · &lt;relative&gt; →` is pitched correctly for what the integration actually knows.

## Critical

- **Hierarchy inversion in the slot card hero (`slot-card.ts:490-518`, styles at `slot-card.styles.ts:178-199`).** The mockup made the **name** the dominant 22px/600 element — anchoring the card's identity. The implementation reduces the name to a 16px/500 form-row labeled `NAME` (small caps), and promotes the **PIN to 22px monospace** as the visual anchor. This inverts the intent: when you open a slot card, the question is "is *Alice's* PIN working?" — Alice should win the visual weight, not the digits. The PIN is what the name *has*. As-is, the PIN dominates and the name reads like a config field. Recommended direction: restore the name to title-class (≈20–22px, 600), drop the `NAME` label entirely (the value below the title chip is self-evident), keep the pencil affordance. Treat the hero as `Name (title) → PIN row → Enabled`, not three equal labeled fields.
- **The `Slot N · {entry_title}` kicker is doing too much in the slot card header (`slot-card.styles.ts:76-85`).** It's currently rendered at `font-size: 18px; font-weight: 500` as `.header-title` — i.e., it has the visual weight of a card title. But it's a *kicker*: orientation metadata. The mockup made this 11px uppercase (`slot-kicker`) so it could co-exist with the dominant name below. Now the layout is `[icon] [Slot 1 · House Locks at 18px] [state chip]` and the actual identity (the name) sits in a small-caps labeled field one row down. Either: (a) demote the kicker to ~11px uppercase and put the name in the header where the title currently is, or (b) accept the new IA but make the name the hero anchor (see above).
- **Collapsible "Conditions" copy is partly stale (the rename to singular is incomplete).**
  - `slot-card-editor.ts:151,203` still labels the toggle and section header as **"Conditions"** (plural).
  - The `collapsed_sections` enum value is still `'conditions'` (plural) in `types.ts`. That one's user-visible only via YAML; renaming would be a breaking change. Probably fine to leave, but flag in docs.
  - The card renders the title as `'Condition'` (singular, `slot-card.ts:583,598`) — so the editor toggle says "Conditions" and the card section says "Condition." That's inconsistent in the UI a user actually sees while editing.
  - The aria-label on the dismiss-error button is fine, but the rendered heading in the dialog is `'Add condition'` (singular, good), while internally the dialog state and class names use `_showConditionDialog` / `Manage Condition` — the user-facing copy should align. Pick one and audit.
- **`&lt;em&gt;&amp;lt;No Name&amp;gt;&lt;/em&gt;` and `&lt;em&gt;&amp;lt;No PIN&amp;gt;&lt;/em&gt;` placeholders (`slot-card.ts:509, 538`)** read like debug output, not empty-state copy. Angle brackets in italics inside a polished hero band is jarring. Recommended: italic gray "Not named" / "No PIN set" without the angle brackets.

## Important

- **The card-level state tinting doubles up the active signal (`slot-card.styles.ts:30-35`).** Active gets the primary-blue gradient on the *whole card background*, AND the state chip says "Active" with a green dot, AND (per the lock card) the state badge is green. The card-level tint says "primary," the chip says "success." Two different positive vocabularies for the same state. Inactive (warning orange tint + warning chip + warning badge) and Disabled (muted + muted + muted) are internally consistent because the tint and chip share a color family. Recommended direction: either (a) make the active card-tint green to match the chip, or (b) make active *neutral* (no tint) and reserve the card-level color for non-default states (inactive/disabled), so the tint becomes a "needs attention" signal rather than another success indicator. Option (b) follows the principle "color the exception, not the norm."
- **State color layering at three intensities is hard to follow.** Slot-card background uses warning at **6% / 4%** opacity, badge dots use **16%**, badge bg uses **16%**, condition overlay uses **8% / 10%**. That's four different opacities for the same color across one card. It's not wrong, but the relationship "lighter = farther back, darker = focal point" isn't crisp. Worth a pass to settle on 2-3 canonical opacity stops and apply them consistently (e.g., 6% for backgrounds, 12% for surfaces, 16% for chips/badges).
- **Slot-card hero `Enabled` toggle has its small-caps label `ENABLED` next to the switch (`slot-card.ts:550`).** A switch is self-describing — a label there is redundant in a way the PIN/Name labels are not (those label values that are otherwise just numbers/text). Compare: `Name: Alice`, `PIN: ••••`, `Enabled: [switch]`. The first two clarify what the value means. The third labels a control that already says what it controls by being a switch in a slot card. Consider dropping the `ENABLED` label.
- **`hero-field-label` `min-width: 56px` (`slot-card.styles.ts:174`) means the Name label, PIN label, and Enabled label all reserve a 56px column** — so the labels visually align. Good in principle. But if the Enabled label is dropped, the PIN's "PIN" label is short enough that 56px reads as a lot of leading whitespace before the PIN value. Re-tune after the label question above is decided.
- **Lock-card slot label is uppercase secondary-text-color at 11px (`lock-codes-card.styles.ts:148-159`).** The slot-card kicker is *not* uppercase and is rendered at 18px (currently as the title). They're both `Slot N · {entry_title}` — but presented in completely different typography. If the slot identifier is shared visual language, treat it as such. The mockup had both at 11px uppercase; the lock card stayed there, the slot card drifted. Pick one rendering.
- **"Last used" without a timestamp shows "Never used" in the meta slot (`slot-card.ts:370-373`)**, but the row still has a clickable "→" that opens more-info on an empty event. Either suppress the affordance arrow when there's no history, or reword to make clear that clicking shows the (empty) event entity.
- **The pencil button next to the name (`slot-card.ts:511-516`) is a separate clickable in addition to the editable name span.** Two click targets do the same thing. The pencil reads as the affordance; the editable span (with hover background) is the redundant one. It's harmless but it's two affordances for one action — and the pencil at `--mdc-icon-size: 14px` is small enough that it's mostly visual noise rather than a real affordance. Consider: name with hover background is the only target, pencil shown only on hover (or removed entirely).
- **Lock-card chip "Active Unmanaged" uses a near-invisible neutral gradient (`lock-codes-card.styles.ts:82-89`) at 6%/2% on text-color rgb.** That's almost identical to the empty chip (3% via `--lcm-section-bg`). At a glance an active unmanaged chip looks like an empty chip. The Managed/Unmanaged identity badge is the only differentiator. Suggest a hint of contrast (slightly stronger neutral, or a subtle warm-gray accent) so an active unmanaged code reads as "occupied" at a glance.
- **`.collapsible-content` has `max-height: 500px` (`shared-styles.ts:325`).** A slot with many helpers (rare but possible) plus a calendar entity row that wraps could overflow this and clip silently. Not a typography issue per se — but discoverable as broken. Either set a much larger ceiling or use a different open mechanism (height: auto with a transition shim).

## Nice-to-have

- **Remove-link warning color (`slot-card.styles.ts:329-348`)** — using warning orange that escalates to error red on hover is a nice gradation. Slightly aggressive for a "clear an optional config field" action; many integrations would treat this as a tertiary text button. That said: the warning treatment matches the orange of a "blocked" condition, which creates a subtle visual rhyme. Probably fine; flag for thought.
- **The slot-card "Add condition" button (`slot-card.styles.ts:357-381`)** is a tinted pill that on hover inverts to filled primary. That's a punchier CTA than its sibling collapsible badges (which are muted by default). It calls attention to itself in the empty state, which is correct intent — it just visually pops more than the surrounding card chrome warrants. Consider lowering hover saturation by one notch.
- **Dialog microcopy (`slot-card.ts:1091-1094`)**: "PIN is active only when this entity is on. Pick a calendar, schedule, binary sensor, switch, or input boolean." The first sentence is great. The second sentence enumerates the technical domains — accurate but not user-facing language. A non-power user reads "binary sensor" and pauses. Consider: "Pick a calendar, schedule, helper, or any on/off entity." Loses one technical correctness for substantial accessibility-of-meaning gain.
- **The `Helpers` sublabel inside Conditions (`slot-card.styles.ts:394-401`)** uses 10px uppercase with letter-spacing 0.08em — same scale as the hero field labels but a smaller font. Consider unifying to the 11px label scale used elsewhere; the 10px is the only one at that size and makes the helpers section feel like a different design system fragment.
- **Card header icon bubble (40×40 key icon at `slot-card.styles.ts:61-74`)** is a strong visual anchor. Nice. But on the lock card, the same 40×40 bubble holds a lock icon (`lock-codes-card.styles.ts:28-42`) — and that's the *card's identity*, while the slot card's bubble is generic (it's "a slot"). Two different semantic loads, one visual. Worth deciding: is the bubble "this is a Lock Code Manager card" (consistent identity) or "what kind of card is this" (varies)? Currently it's neither — slot card has a key, lock card has a lock, both at the same size, but only one is meaningful. Tend toward making it meaningful: maybe slot card's bubble shows the active/blocked/disabled state via icon (key / clock / lock-off).

## Questions

- **Is the slot card hero meant to be a "form" or a "summary"?** The mockup's hero was a summary (PIN + Enable, name above as a header). The implementation pushed the name *into* the hero as a labeled field, making the whole band read as a form. If it's intentional ("we want all editable fields in one band, with consistent label affordance"), the decision is defensible — but then the *card title* needs to be re-established somewhere because the small-caps label + 16px value isn't doing it. If it's not intentional, see the Critical hierarchy item.
- **Should the active state get card-level tinting at all?** The mockup did not (it kept active cards on the default card background). The implementation added a subtle blue gradient. This was added for cross-card consistency with the lock chip's active-managed treatment — but on the slot card (a single-slot focused view), a tint says "different from default," which active isn't. Worth deciding: is "this slot is healthy" worth coloring the whole card? In a list, sure. In a focused view, maybe not.
- **The condition overlay status emojis (`slot-card.ts:672`)** use raw `✓` / `✗` characters in the overlay status. Adjacent code uses MDI svg icons. Consistency-of-icon-source question: should those be `mdiCheck` / `mdiClose` for visual parity with the rest of the card chrome?

## Mockup divergence summary

- **Name typography demoted from 22px/600 hero to 16px/500 labeled field** — biggest divergence; flagged Critical above.
- **Header gained a 40×40 icon bubble** that's not in the mockup. Reasonable for cross-card consistency with the lock card; surfaces the slot identity visually.
- **Card-level state tinting added** (mockup left active cards untinted). See Question above.
- **Conditions section header is `Condition` (singular)** in the card body but `Conditions` (plural) in the editor — see Critical.
- **Manage Condition link became Remove condition + auto-commit picker** — intentional iteration past the mockup; the dialog footer with Cancel/Save/Remove is gone. This is a real improvement; flag and keep.
- **`Condition` collapsed-state summary uses an inline badge** (`slot-card.ts:622-632`) showing `✓ {entity name}` or `✗ {entity name}` in the badge slot. The mockup put this in the right-aligned summary column with `ok`/`warn` color. The current implementation's badge uses primary-blue tint when allowing (not green) and warning-yellow when blocking — losing the green/amber traffic-light pattern the rest of the card uses. Consider switching the allowing badge to use the success-color treatment so all "good" states use green and all "needs attention" states use warning.

---

<task-notification>
<task-id>a06febcf7af1dc9f9</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01QdEoSV4BukPRdMmVeMGgC2</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-raman-projects-lock-code-manager/71c16e9b-de1a-419b-a20c-1b5f51ff2253/tasks/a06febcf7af1dc9f9.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Code quality review of PR #1116" completed</summary>
<result>The empty-string-name corner case appears in a commit on this PR (`b1bd00c5`), but the actual behavior when `slot.name === ''` would be:
- `slotName = ''.trim() = ''`
- `class="slot-name unnamed"` (correct)
- text content is `''` — rendered as empty space (the user sees nothing)

This is intended-ish since `showName` is gated by `!!slotName || managed !== false`, so for unmanaged slots with no name, `showName` is false. For managed slots with `slot.name === ''`, `showName=true` and it renders empty content. The "Unnamed" placeholder would never appear because `??` doesn't trigger on `''`. Cosmetically minor — probably worth using `||` instead of `??`, but low impact since slot names are validated upstream.

I have enough to provide my review.

---

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `npm test` | 644 tests passed (15 files, ~1.3s) |
| `npm run build` | Built successfully (only the pre-existing `_config possibly undefined` TS warnings the brief said to ignore) |
| `uv run pytest tests/ --timeout=30 -q` | 810 passed in 18.08s |
| `prek run --all-files` | All hooks Passed |

# ❌ ISSUES

## IMPORTANT

### 1. Name-edit auto-focus selector never matches the rendered DOM

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/ts/slot-card.ts:240-252`
**Severity:** IMPORTANT (confidence ~92)

The `updated()` lifecycle picks the focus selector from this map:

```ts
const selectors: Record&lt;string, string&gt; = {
    name: '.name .name-edit-input',
    pin: '.hero-pin .pin-edit-input'
};
```

The PIN input is inside `&lt;div class="hero-field hero-pin"&gt;` (line 520), so `.hero-pin .pin-edit-input` resolves correctly.

The name input, however, is rendered as:

```ts
&lt;div class="hero-field"&gt;                          // line 493 — NO ".name" class
  &lt;span class="hero-field-label"&gt;Name&lt;/span&gt;
  ...
  &lt;input class="edit-input name-edit-input" ... /&gt; // line 497
&lt;/div&gt;
```

There is no ancestor with class `name` anywhere in the hero (only `hero-name-value` and `hero-name-pencil`), so `shadowRoot.querySelector('.name .name-edit-input')` returns `null` every time the user enters name-edit mode, and the new field never auto-focuses. PIN-edit auto-focus works; name-edit does not.

There are no tests covering focus behavior (`grep` for "focus" in `slot-card.integration.test.ts` returns only the file header), which is why this slipped through.

**Fix:** Either drop the `.name` ancestor or add the class to the wrapper. Simplest is to drop the ancestor since `.name-edit-input` is unique:

```ts
const selectors: Record&lt;string, string&gt; = {
    name: '.name-edit-input',
    pin: '.pin-edit-input'
};
```

(or equivalently `.hero-field .name-edit-input` to mirror the PIN pattern). Add a regression test that mounts the card, enters name-edit mode via `_startEditing('name')`, awaits `updateComplete`, and asserts `shadowRoot.activeElement` matches the input.

---

# ✅ NICE-TO-HAVE (low confidence — for your judgement, no action required)

### N1. Empty-string `slot.name` falls through `??` and renders an empty span

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/ts/lock-codes-card.ts:548-550`

`slotName = slot.name?.trim()` collapses `""` to `""`. The class clause `${slotName ? '' : 'unnamed'}` correctly applies the `unnamed` modifier, but `${slotName ?? 'Unnamed'}` does not, since `??` ignores `""`. The span renders empty instead of showing the "Unnamed" placeholder. The case is unlikely (managed slots normally have a non-empty name) but the two clauses are inconsistent. If you want to fix it: change `??` to `||` on line 549. (This was introduced/touched by `b1bd00c5` on this PR but is cosmetic.)

### N2. Backend `entry_title = None` branch when entry is missing has no test

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/websocket.py:488-492`

```python
entry_id = slot_ids.config_entry_id if slot_ids else None
entry_title = None
if entry_id:
    entry = hass.config_entries.async_get_entry(entry_id)
    entry_title = entry.title if entry else None
```

The defensive `entry is None` branch (entry-removed race) is reachable but not exercised by any test. `_serialize_slot` correctly omits the field when title is falsy. Adding a test would be defensive only, not closing a real gap.

### N3. Both `_handlePickerChange` and `ha-dialog @closed` invoke `_closeConditionDialog`, which is idempotent

Just an observation: `_commitConditionPick` calls `_closeConditionDialog()` directly, and that triggers `ha-dialog`'s `closed` event, which fires the same handler again. Both calls are safe (idempotent state assignment), and the test on line 3551 explicitly proves `_dialogSaving` is preserved across close. No fix needed; calling out so future readers don't add a redundant guard.

---

# Notes on areas asked about (no issues found)

- **`_handlePickerChange` / `_commitConditionPick` / `_removeCondition` flow** — re-entry guards in place; `_dialogSaving` is reset in both success and catch branches; close-without-reset behavior is documented and tested (`it('_closeConditionDialog does NOT reset _dialogSaving')`).
- **`_renderConditionsSection` branching** — three states (no entity + no helpers, no entity + helpers, has entity) handled correctly.
- **`_startEditing('pin')` + `_exitPinEdit`** — `_revealedForEdit` is only set when reveal had to be flipped from false; manual reveal followed by pencil click does not set the flag (correct); resubscribe failure reverts both `_revealed` and `_revealedForEdit` and surfaces an error (covered by C2 tests).
- **`_renderEventRow`** — guards on `!eventEntityId`, missing from `hass.states`, and `unavailable` state are all in place; `_navigateToEventHistory` mirrors them as defense-in-depth (covered by tests at lines 3323 and 3339).
- **`_renderConditionDialog` Add-only** — no leftover `_dialogMode` references in the source.
- **Lock card `_getCodeClass` two-state off/pending** — `enabled === false` → `off`, otherwise `pending` (defensive default for unknown enabled). `isPending` detection correctly splits on space (handles `pending masked` suffix).
- **Lock card `_renderSlotChip`** — `slot-top` + `slot-content-row` + dot on state badge all match the test fixtures (covered by tests at line 1194, 1209, 1224).
- **Backend `_serialize_lock_coordinator` config_entry_title lookup** — handles missing `slot_ids`, missing `entry_id`, and missing `entry` paths.
- **`_actionErrorTimer` cleanup** — set in `_setActionError` (clears prior timer first), cleared in `disconnectedCallback`, self-clears on fire. No leak.
- **`_entityRowCache`** — bounded by the number of distinct condition + helper entity IDs in card config; error placeholders intentionally not cached so subsequent renders retry (tested at line 3400).
- **Project conventions** — no inline imports, no `nonlocal`, no Args/Returns docstrings, no caplog text assertions added, no acronym abbreviations introduced. New backend test docstrings are prose-style (good).

---

**Verdict:** Approve once the name-edit focus selector bug (Issue 1) is fixed. Everything else is either tested correctly, cosmetic, or pre-existing. The test coverage added for the new code paths (event row, condition dialog re-entry, PIN-edit reveal lifecycle, `_getEntityRow` error handling, lock card state badge dot, `config_entry_title`) is thorough.

Relevant files:
- `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/ts/slot-card.ts`
- `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/ts/lock-codes-card.ts`
- `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts`
- `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/websocket.py`
- `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/tests/test_websocket.py`</result>
